D.Engage.history: D.Engage (dengage.com) was founded in 2018 as an omnichannel customer experience and marketing automation platform. It has backing from TIBAS Ventures, the VC arm of Isbank (Turkey's largest private bank), reflecting its Turkish operational roots despite London headquarters.
D.Engage.summary_abuse: D.Engage is a marketing automation and bulk-messaging platform; like most email/SMS automation tools it could theoretically be misused for spam campaigns, but targets enterprise clients with formal onboarding. No specific documented abuse incidents found.

D3.history: D3.js was created in 2011 by Mike Bostock, Jeff Heer, and Vadim Ogievetsky at Stanford University, as a successor to their earlier Protovis library (2009). Bostock subsequently worked as a graphics editor at The New York Times. In 2020, Bostock co-founded Observable, a data visualization notebook platform which became D3 home. Observable was acquired by Databricks in 2024.
D3.summary_abuse: D3.js is a foundational JavaScript data visualization library with no consumer-facing surface. It is used to build custom charts and graphics in browsers and has no abuse signal whatsoever.

Dable.history: Dable was founded in 2015 in Seoul, South Korea as an AI-powered content discovery and native advertising platform for media publishers across Asia. In January 2021 the company raised $12M in Series C funding at a $90M valuation to expand globally.
Dable.summary_abuse: Dable is a native advertising and content recommendation network with elevated tracking density from serving behavioral ad targeting across publisher sites. Native advertising networks carry some risk of serving malvertising, but no significant specific abuse incidents have been reported for Dable.

Dachser.history: Dachser was founded in 1930 by Thomas Dachser in Kempten, Germany as a trucking company. The privately-owned family business has grown into one of Europe's largest logistics companies with global operations, remaining family-owned for nearly a century.
Dachser.summary_abuse: Dachser is a major European freight and logistics operator with no notable abuse signal. Like other parcel carriers, it may be occasionally impersonated in phishing campaigns, but its own platform has no consumer-facing surface exploitable by fraudsters.

Daily Deals.history: Daily Deals is a Shopify-focused flash sale and countdown timer app from Brio Commerce, serving e-commerce merchants with timed promotions. Specific founding year not publicly confirmed.
Daily Deals.summary_abuse: Daily Deals is a Shopify discount and flash-sale plugin with no notable abuse signal. It is a merchant-facing tool that helps e-commerce stores run promotions and does not provide consumer-facing content hosting.

DailyKarma.history: DailyKarma was founded around 2018 in Santa Monica, California as a cause-marketing platform enabling e-commerce merchants to incorporate charitable donations into their purchase flow.
DailyKarma.summary_abuse: DailyKarma is a cause-marketing plugin for e-commerce with no notable abuse signal. It facilitates charitable donations at checkout and has no content-hosting or user-generated content features that fraudsters would exploit.

Dailymotion.history: Dailymotion was founded in March 2005 in Paris by Benjamin Bejbaum and Olivier Poitrey. Orange (France Telecom) became majority owner, then sold 80% to Vivendi in 2015 for €217M. Vivendi later restructured and Dailymotion passed to Canal+ (a Vivendi subsidiary). It remains one of the world's larger video-sharing platforms outside of YouTube.
Dailymotion.summary_abuse: Dailymotion is a major video-sharing platform that allows users to upload content, creating a meaningful abuse surface for pirated content and occasionally inappropriate or copyright-infringing material. The platform has faced DMCA takedown requests and copyright claims. Its free, open video-hosting model is exploited for piracy at a lower frequency than YouTube due to smaller traffic, but vendor enforcement exists through ContentID-style systems. Not a primary tool for phishing but piracy abuse is documented.

daisyUI.history: daisyUI was created by Pouya Saadeghi and released in 2021 as a Tailwind CSS component library. It gained rapid adoption and became one of the most popular Tailwind CSS plugin/component libraries.
daisyUI.summary_abuse: daisyUI is a Tailwind CSS component library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure frontend styling tool with no server-side surface.

Dakno.history: Dakno Marketing was founded around 2003 by Brad Carroll in Raleigh, North Carolina as a real estate lead generation and website marketing platform. It remains a small independent company serving real estate agents and brokers.
Dakno.summary_abuse: Dakno is a real estate lead generation and website platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves licensed real estate professionals and has no public content-hosting surface exploitable by fraudsters.

Daktela Omnichannel.history: Daktela was founded in 2005 in Prague, Czech Republic as a cloud-based contact centre platform. It has grown into a mid-size Central European CCaaS vendor serving omnichannel customer service operations.
Daktela Omnichannel.summary_abuse: Daktela is a contact centre SaaS platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves enterprise and SMB customer service teams and has no consumer-facing content surface that fraudsters would target.

Dalue.history: Dalue is a cloud-based search engine developed in South Korea enabling information retrieval and indexing across online data sources. Limited public information is available about the company's history.
Dalue.summary_abuse: Dalue is a niche cloud-based search engine from South Korea with no documented abuse signal.

DanAds.history: DanAds was founded in 2013 in Malmo, Sweden as a self-serve advertising technology platform enabling publishers to automate ad sales and creative management. The company is backed by Danir and ranked among Deloitte Sweden Technology Fast 50.
DanAds.summary_abuse: DanAds is a publisher-side ad tech platform for automating ad sales; as with other ad networks, it faces typical malvertising risks if advertiser vetting is insufficient, but no specific documented abuse incidents were found.

Dancer.history: Dancer (also called Dancer2) is a lightweight Perl web application framework inspired by Ruby's Sinatra, created in 2009 by Alexis Sukrieh. It was redesigned as Dancer2 by Sawyer X around 2012 to address architectural limitations of the original. It is maintained by a community of Perl developers.
Dancer.summary_abuse: Dancer is a Perl web framework with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is a developer tool used to build web applications and presents no direct fraud or abuse risk.

DanDomain.history: DanDomain was founded in 1999 in Skanderborg, Denmark as a hosting and domain registration company serving Danish SMBs. Combell (Belgium) acquired DanDomain in 2016, and team.blue subsequently acquired Combell in 2019, making DanDomain part of the pan-European team.blue hosting group.
DanDomain.summary_abuse: DanDomain is a Danish hosting and domain registrar. Domain registrars and hosting providers are occasionally used to register phishing or scam domains, but DanDomain serves primarily local Danish SMBs and there is no notable documented abuse pattern specific to the platform.

DanDomain Webshop.history: DanDomain Webshop is the e-commerce arm of DanDomain, a Danish hosting company founded in 1999. DanDomain was acquired by Combell in 2016 and later by team.blue in 2019, making the webshop platform part of the European team.blue group.
DanDomain Webshop.summary_abuse: DanDomain Webshop is a hosted e-commerce platform for Danish SMBs with no notable abuse signal. Like any e-commerce platform it could potentially host scam storefronts, but the platform is targeted at local Danish businesses with standard KYC.

Danneo CMS.history: Danneo CMS is a Russian open-source PHP/MySQL content management system. It was developed as a self-hosted website building tool primarily targeting the Russian-speaking market. The project appears to have low maintenance activity.
Danneo CMS.summary_abuse: Danneo CMS is a self-hosted Russian CMS built on PHP and MySQL. As a self-hosted CMS with limited development activity, older installations may have unpatched vulnerabilities that could be exploited for phishing or malware hosting. The Russian origin limits its GDPR compatibility and Western abuse-response mechanisms.

Daphne.history: Daphne is the ASGI (Asynchronous Server Gateway Interface) server created by the Django Software Foundation, originally developed by Andrew Godwin around 2015 as part of the Django Channels project to add WebSocket and async support to Django applications. It is maintained under the Django umbrella and is the official reference ASGI server for Django.
Daphne.summary_abuse: Daphne is the ASGI server component for Django with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is purely a backend web server component used by Python/Django developers.

Dapta.history: Dapta was founded in 2023 in Phoenix, Arizona as an AI-powered no-code automation and personalization platform for SMB marketing and sales workflows.
Dapta.summary_abuse: Dapta is a new AI-powered marketing automation and personalization platform with no documented abuse signal.

Darkmode.js.history: Darkmode.js is a lightweight MIT-licensed JavaScript library created by Sandoche Adittane (sandoche) around 2019 that adds a dark mode toggle to websites using a CSS mix-blend-mode overlay technique. Hosted on GitHub, it gained initial traction via ProductHunt.
Darkmode.js.summary_abuse: Darkmode.js is a small client-side JavaScript utility with no server component and no abuse risk.

Dart.history: Dart was created by Lars Bak and Kasper Lund at Google, announced at the GOTO conference in October 2011. Initially designed as a replacement for JavaScript, Dart 1.0 was released in November 2013. Dart 2.0 in 2018 introduced a sound type system. Dart became the primary language for Flutter, Google's cross-platform UI framework launched in 2018, dramatically increasing its adoption.
Dart.summary_abuse: Dart is a general-purpose programming language created by Google with no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal.

Darwin.history: Darwin is the open-source UNIX-like core of Apple's operating systems, released publicly by Apple in 2000 as the foundation for macOS (then called Mac OS X). Darwin combines the Mach microkernel, BSD kernel subsystems, and various Apple-developed components. It forms the basis for macOS, iOS, watchOS, and tvOS. Apple maintains Darwin under various open-source licenses including the Apple Public Source License.
Darwin.summary_abuse: Darwin is the open-source operating system core from Apple and has no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal. It is a foundational operating system component.

Darwin Pricing.history: Darwin Pricing was founded in 2013 by Sebastien Fauvel in Basel, Switzerland as a dynamic pricing and geo-targeted pricing optimization platform for online retailers.
Darwin Pricing.summary_abuse: Darwin Pricing is a dynamic pricing optimization tool for e-commerce retailers with no notable abuse signal. It helps merchants adjust prices based on market conditions and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Dashly.history: Dashly was started in 2014 and grew into a conversational marketing and live chat platform. It is incorporated in Delaware, USA and serves B2B SaaS companies with chatbots, email automation and lead management tools.
Dashly.summary_abuse: Dashly is a conversational marketing and live chat platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool for engaging website visitors and has no public content-hosting features exploitable for fraud.

data nugget.history: data nugget is a brand analytics and growth platform for identifying marketing opportunities; no founding year or notable ownership history found in public records.
data nugget.summary_abuse: data nugget is a marketing analytics SaaS with no consumer-facing surface. No documented abuse patterns have been reported.

Data8.history: Data8 was founded in 2005 in Chester, United Kingdom as a data quality management and validation platform. The company remains an independent B2B SaaS vendor serving UK businesses with address verification, phone validation and data enrichment services.
Data8.summary_abuse: Data8 is a B2B data quality and validation API service with no notable abuse signal. It processes address and contact data for businesses and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable for fraud.

Databuddy.history: Databuddy is a privacy-first analytics platform for developers, founded around 2020 by Issa Nassar. It offers a lightweight alternative to Google Analytics with built-in privacy compliance. The company is incorporated in the UK.
Databuddy.summary_abuse: Databuddy is a privacy-first web analytics tool with no notable abuse signal. Its backend analytics infrastructure has no consumer-facing surface or content-hosting features that fraudsters would target.

Datacrush.history: Datacrush is a marketing automation platform based in Latin America (Argentina). Limited public information is available about its founding date and history.
Datacrush.summary_abuse: Datacrush is a small marketing automation platform with no notable documented abuse signal.

Datadog.history: Datadog was founded in 2010 in New York City by Olivier Pomel and Alexis Lê-Quôc, who previously worked together at Wireless Generation. The company built a cloud-scale infrastructure monitoring and observability platform. Datadog went public on Nasdaq in September 2019, raising $648 million at an $8.7 billion valuation. The stock price rose ~37% on its first trading day. By 2024, Datadog had ~6,500 employees in 33 countries serving over 28,000 customers including a significant portion of the Fortune 500, with annual revenue exceeding $2 billion.
Datadog.summary_abuse: Datadog is a publicly traded enterprise infrastructure monitoring platform with no notable abuse history. The company holds SOC 2 Type 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and PCI-DSS certifications and maintains a public security disclosure program. As a monitoring-focused backend SaaS, it presents minimal abuse risk.

DataDojo.history: DataDojo was founded around 2022 as an e-commerce customer data platform focused on visitor identification. The company targets direct-to-consumer brands with a tracking pixel that identifies anonymous website visitors and enriches them with contact data for retargeting. DataDojo claims to identify up to 70% of site visitors and is used by 140+ e-commerce brands. The platform integrates with Shopify, Klaviyo, and Meta Ads.
DataDojo.summary_abuse: DataDojo operates in a privacy-sensitive space by identifying and profiling anonymous website visitors without explicit consent, which raises GDPR and CCPA compliance concerns. The platform's core value proposition is tracking visitors who have not provided explicit consent for identification, which may conflict with privacy regulations. This represents a moderate abuse risk, particularly in European markets.

DataDome.history: DataDome was founded in 2015 in Paris, France by Benjamin Fabre, Fabien Grenier, Benjamin Barrier, and Olivier Trabucato. The company was created after the founders observed that most organizations were unable to detect even basic bot attacks. DataDome uses AI and machine learning to process over 3 trillion signals daily to detect and block bot attacks, credential stuffing, account takeover fraud, and web scraping in real time. The company raised $82M total across multiple rounds including a $42M Series C in March 2023, protecting over 300 enterprise customers globally.
DataDome.summary_abuse: DataDome is a reputable bot protection and fraud prevention platform with no known abuse history. The company's core mission is to protect enterprises from malicious bots, and it operates with strong security practices including SOC2 certification. No reports of data misuse or malicious activity found.

DataGrail.history: DataGrail was founded in 2018 in San Francisco, California as a data privacy automation platform. The company raised $84M+ across five rounds, including a $45M Series C in October 2022 led by Third Point Ventures.
DataGrail.summary_abuse: DataGrail is a privacy compliance and data rights automation platform with no notable abuse signal. It helps companies manage GDPR and CCPA compliance and processes data on behalf of its enterprise clients; no consumer-facing surface for fraud.

DataLife Engine.history: DataLife Engine (DLE) is a Russian commercial CMS developed by SoftNews Media Group, with development beginning in 2004. It was built on the CutePHP system foundation and targets media outlets, news portals, and blogs. Written in PHP with a MySQL database, DLE became popular primarily in Russian-speaking countries. By 2014, it ranked 4th most popular CMS in the .ru zone with approximately 59,900 installations, though the majority were reportedly pirated 'zeroed' versions. The software is sold as a one-time license purchase.
DataLife Engine.summary_abuse: DataLife Engine is a Russian-developed CMS with a moderate abuse risk profile. The software is widely pirated and many installations run unauthorized versions. As a Russian software product, it lacks GDPR compliance infrastructure and operates outside typical Western security frameworks. The high rate of pirated installations (majority according to tracking data) also suggests an ecosystem with loose control over software distribution.

Datalitics.history: Datalitics is a Brazilian lead tracking and customer acquisition analytics platform. Limited public information is available about its founding date and history.
Datalitics.summary_abuse: Datalitics is a small lead-tracking and analytics platform with no notable documented abuse signal.

DataMilk.history: DataMilk is an AI-driven UI optimization and conversion rate platform for e-commerce. Limited public history is available about its founding date.
DataMilk.summary_abuse: DataMilk is an AI conversion optimization tool for e-commerce with no notable abuse signal. It autonomously adjusts UI elements on merchant sites and has no public content-hosting surface for fraud.

Datanyze.history: Datanyze was founded in 2012 as a B2B technographic and sales intelligence platform. In September 2018, ZoomInfo acquired Datanyze for $15M. The platform now operates as a subsidiary of ZoomInfo, offering a browser extension for contact data and technographic enrichment.
Datanyze.summary_abuse: Datanyze is a sales intelligence and B2B contact-data tool now part of ZoomInfo. Contact-data platforms can be misused for spam prospecting, but Datanyze targets legitimate sales teams and operates under ZoomInfo's compliance framework. No specific documented abuse incidents.

Datarize.history: Datarize was founded in 2019 in Seoul, South Korea by Seongmoo Kim and Gyumin Lee as an AI-powered e-commerce CRM and personalized marketing platform. The company raised $11M in Series B funding.
Datarize.summary_abuse: Datarize is an AI-driven e-commerce CRM platform with no notable abuse signal. It processes customer behavioral data for marketing personalization with no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Datascape.history: Datascape is a cloud-based ERP solution for New Zealand local government, developed by Datacom, one of Australasia's largest IT companies. It is a vertical ERP product serving councils and public sector organizations.
Datascape.summary_abuse: Datascape is a local government ERP platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B government software solution with no consumer-facing surface.

Dataships.history: Dataships is a data privacy compliance platform helping e-commerce businesses manage GDPR and global data privacy regulations. Limited public founding history is available.
Dataships.summary_abuse: Dataships is a data privacy compliance tool with no notable abuse signal. It helps merchants maintain compliance with data protection regulations and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Datastar.history: Datastar is an open-source hypermedia framework for building reactive web applications, developed by the starfederation organization on GitHub. It is written primarily in TypeScript and positions itself as a lightweight alternative to React — enabling server-side rendering with real-time frontend reactivity without a virtual DOM or complex build tooling. The project accumulated 4,400+ GitHub stars and released over 33 versions, reaching v1.0.1 in April 2026. Licensed under MIT.
Datastar.summary_abuse: Datastar is an open-source frontend framework with no known abuse history. As a client-side library with no data collection or hosting component, it poses negligible abuse risk.

DataTables.history: DataTables is a jQuery table plugin created by Allan Jardine of SpryMedia, first released in 2008. Jardine developed it after finding that existing table plugins did not meet his project requirements. The plugin provides advanced interaction controls such as sorting, filtering, and pagination for HTML tables. It has grown to become one of the most widely used table libraries on the web, with SpryMedia later building a commercial support and extensions business around it. Licensed under MIT.
DataTables.summary_abuse: DataTables is a widely used open-source jQuery table library with no notable abuse history. As a frontend UI library with no server-side component or data collection, it poses negligible abuse risk.

Datatrics.history: Datatrics was founded in 2012 in the Netherlands as a data-driven marketing and customer data platform. It was acquired by Growens (an Italian publicly-listed company) in 2018, then sold to Spotler Group via its Squeezely subsidiary in October 2023 for €1.66M.
Datatrics.summary_abuse: Datatrics is a marketing CDP platform with no notable abuse signal. It processes customer data for personalization campaigns on behalf of e-commerce merchants and has no public-facing content hosting features.

DatHuis.history: DatHuis is a Dutch real estate marketing automation platform. Limited public information is available about its founding date and history.
DatHuis.summary_abuse: DatHuis is a real estate marketing automation tool serving Dutch property professionals with no notable abuse signal.

DatoCMS.history: DatoCMS was founded in 2015 in Florence, Italy, originally as an internal tool at an Italian web agency. It grew into a commercially available headless CMS targeting developers and agencies building Jamstack and static sites.
DatoCMS.summary_abuse: DatoCMS is a headless CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer-facing API-based content platform with no direct consumer-facing content hosting that fraudsters would exploit.

Daxko.history: Daxko was founded in 1998 in Birmingham, Alabama as a member management and payment processing platform for health and wellness organizations, particularly YMCAs, JCCs, and Boys and Girls Clubs. The company has maintained a 25+ year partnership with the YMCA movement.
Daxko.summary_abuse: Daxko is a member management platform for non-profit fitness and wellness organizations with no notable abuse signal. It serves YMCAs and similar institutions and handles membership data rather than hosting public-facing content.

Day.js.history: Day.js was created in 2018 by GitHub user iamkun (Yang Fang) as a 2kB immutable date-time library alternative to Moment.js. It was created in response to community demand for a smaller, immutable, API-compatible replacement for Moment.js, which suffered from bundle bloat (300kB+ minified) and mutability side effects. Day.js quickly gained widespread adoption and became one of the most downloaded npm packages, with the Moment.js team later officially recommending Day.js as a successor. Licensed under MIT.
Day.js.summary_abuse: Day.js is a foundational open-source date manipulation library with no abuse surface. As a pure client/server-side utility library with no data collection, network calls, or hosting component, it poses zero abuse risk.

db-ip.history: db-ip (dbip) is a geolocation API and database service providing IP address geolocation data. Founded around 2013. It competes in the IP intelligence market alongside services like MaxMind and ipapi.
db-ip.summary_abuse: db-ip is an IP geolocation service with no notable direct abuse signal. IP geolocation data can be used to profile website visitors, which raises privacy considerations, but the service itself is not primarily associated with fraud or phishing infrastructure.

dc.js.history: dc.js is a multi-dimensional charting library built on top of d3.js and crossfilter, created around 2012. It is an open-source project used for interactive data analysis dashboards.
dc.js.summary_abuse: dc.js is a charting library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure frontend data visualization tool.

DCKAP Commerce.history: DCKAP was founded in 2005 in Round Rock, Texas, USA, initially as a digital solutions company. Over time it expanded into B2B ecommerce, launching DCKAP Commerce as a dedicated platform for distributors and manufacturers. No notable ownership changes.
DCKAP Commerce.summary_abuse: DCKAP Commerce is a B2B ecommerce platform for distributors and manufacturers with no notable abuse signal. It targets enterprise wholesale buyers rather than anonymous consumers and has no public content-hosting surface.

DDoS-Guard.history: DDoS-Guard was founded in 2011, first registered in Ukraine before relocating to Sevastopol by Evgeny Marchenko and Dmitry Sabitov after Ukrainian authorities began investigating the company for hosting Verified, a credit card fraud forum. The company provides DDoS protection and web hosting to clients including the Russian Ministry of Defence, the alt-tech network Parler, and numerous sites flagged by security researchers for criminal content. In December 2022, the European Commission added DDoS-Guard to its Counterfeit and Piracy Watch List.
DDoS-Guard.summary_abuse: DDoS-Guard has a well-documented history of hosting clients engaged in criminal activity, including phishing sites, cybercrime forums, carding operations, and doxxing campaigns. Security researcher Brian Krebs reported in 2021 that a vast number of DDoS-Guard-hosted websites are phishing sites or tied to cybercrime services. The company has repeatedly acknowledged hosting abusive clients, describing itself as politically neutral and demanding legal proof before actioning abuse reports. The European Commission listed it on the Counterfeit and Piracy Watch List in 2022. DDoS-Guard is considered a significant safe harbor for threat actors seeking infrastructure resistant to takedown.

Deadline Funnel.history: Deadline Funnel was founded in 2015 by Jack Born as an email marketing and urgency automation tool that creates authentic countdown timers tied to individual subscribers. The company remains an independent bootstrapped SaaS.
Deadline Funnel.summary_abuse: Deadline Funnel is an urgency marketing tool used to create time-limited offers. It can be misused to create false scarcity in fraudulent sales funnels, but the platform itself serves legitimate marketers and does not provide free hosting or anonymous signups that enable mass phishing.

Deal.ai.history: Deal.ai is an AI-powered white-label platform for marketing content creation and business growth. Limited public founding history is available; the platform appears to be a recent AI-era product launched post-2022.
Deal.ai.summary_abuse: Deal.ai is explicitly a white-label platform that resellers use to build AI-powered marketing services. White-label AI platforms carry risk of being used to create fake content farms or spam infrastructure, but no specific documented abuse incidents have been found.

Dealer Spike.history: Dealer Spike was founded in February 2008 in Lake Oswego, Oregon as a digital marketing and website solutions provider for powersports, marine, trailer, and equipment dealerships.
Dealer Spike.summary_abuse: Dealer Spike is a vertical B2B digital marketing platform for dealerships with no notable abuse signal. It serves licensed dealers and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Dealspotr.history: Dealspotr was founded around 2014-2015 by Michael Quoc in Los Angeles, California as a crowdsourced coupon and deal discovery community enabling users to share and find discount codes.
Dealspotr.summary_abuse: Dealspotr is a coupon aggregation and deal-sharing platform that collects promo codes from users. Coupon platforms can be used to publish fake or expired deals to drive traffic, and third-party tracking is moderate given the affiliate marketing context. No major documented fraud incidents, but the open deal-submission model carries some risk of spammy or misleading content.

DearDoc.history: DearDoc is a healthcare-focused appointment scheduling and patient communication platform. Limited public founding history is available; the platform appears to target medical practices.
DearDoc.summary_abuse: DearDoc is a healthcare appointment scheduling platform serving medical practices with no notable abuse signal. It handles patient appointment data and is not a content-hosting surface for fraud.

Debian.history: Debian was founded by Ian Murdock on August 16, 1993. The name combines 'Debra' (his then-girlfriend) and 'Ian'. Murdock created it in response to poor maintenance of the SLS Linux distribution. Debian introduced the concept of a community-governed distribution managed through democratic processes and the Debian Social Contract. It became one of the most influential Linux distributions, forming the base for hundreds of derivatives including Ubuntu. Ian Murdock passed away in 2015. Debian is maintained by the Debian Project, a global volunteer organization, and is sponsored by Software in the Public Interest (SPI). It is the second-oldest Linux distribution still in active development after Slackware.
Debian.summary_abuse: Debian is a foundational open-source Linux distribution with no notable abuse history as a technology. As a general-purpose OS distributed freely, it can be used for any purpose including by malicious actors, but this is true of all operating systems. The Debian Project maintains a strong security team and rapid vulnerability response process.

Decap CMS.history: Decap CMS began in 2016 as Netlify CMS, created by Netlify to provide a Git-based editorial UI for static site generators. For years it was the de facto standard for Git-based content management. In February 2023, Netlify transferred ownership to the open-source community under the decaporg GitHub organization and rebranded it as Decap CMS. Stewardship shifted from Netlify to PM TechHub, a Slovenian agency, as the primary community maintainer. The codebase and config format remain unchanged. Licensed under MIT.
Decap CMS.summary_abuse: Decap CMS is an open-source Git-based CMS with no known abuse history. As a self-hosted tool that stores content in the user's own Git repository, it has no central data collection or hosting surface that could be abused.

Decibel.history: Decibel was founded in 2014 by Ben Harris and Timothy de Paris in London, UK as a digital experience analytics platform. In March 2021, Medallia acquired Decibel for approximately €134M to expand its digital analytics capabilities.
Decibel.summary_abuse: Decibel is a digital behavioral analytics platform for enterprise websites, now part of Medallia, with no notable abuse signal. It captures session replays and user behavioral data for UX analysis, operating under enterprise privacy agreements.

decimal.js.history: decimal.js is a JavaScript arbitrary-precision decimal library created around 2014. It is widely used in financial and scientific applications requiring precise decimal arithmetic.
decimal.js.summary_abuse: decimal.js is a JavaScript arithmetic library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure utility library with no consumer-facing surface.

Deco.cx.history: Deco.cx was founded around 2022 in Brazil as an enterprise frontend platform for e-commerce, offering edge-native composable architecture to accelerate storefront performance.
Deco.cx.summary_abuse: Deco.cx is an enterprise frontend and e-commerce platform with no notable abuse signal. It provides infrastructure for merchant storefronts rather than hosting arbitrary user-generated content.

DedeCMS.history: DedeCMS (Dream Weaving) is a Chinese open-source CMS developed by DreamSoft, operated by Shanghai Zhuozhuo Network Technology Co., Ltd. Development began around 2004, making it one of China's oldest and most-used PHP CMS platforms. Written in PHP with MySQL, it targets Chinese individual webmasters, small businesses, and news portals. The CMS has historically been subject to multiple SQL injection and remote code execution vulnerabilities, and has long had a problematic security track record among Chinese web infrastructure.
DedeCMS.summary_abuse: DedeCMS has a documented history of security vulnerabilities including SQL injection and remote code execution flaws, which have been exploited in the wild to compromise websites. Many DedeCMS installations have been used as vectors for malware distribution, SEO spam, and website defacement. The platform is widely used in the Chinese web ecosystem but its security track record is poor. Researchers have documented significant exploitation of DedeCMS vulnerabilities for spreading malware and generating spam traffic.

Deep Lawn.history: Deep Lawn is a sales and quoting tool purpose-built for lawn care and pest control companies. Limited public history is available about its founding date.
Deep Lawn.summary_abuse: Deep Lawn is a niche vertical SaaS for lawn care businesses with no notable abuse signal.

DeepAR.history: DeepAR was founded in 2018 in London, UK as an augmented reality SDK enabling developers to add AR face filters, 3D effects, and background removal to mobile and web apps. In April 2025, Zalando (the European fashion platform) acquired DeepAR.
DeepAR.summary_abuse: DeepAR is an AR SDK for developer integration with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B technology tool for building AR features and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

DeepMarkit.history: DeepMarkit Corp. is a Canadian technology company traded on the TSX Venture Exchange and OTC markets (OTC: MKTDF). The company focused on building MintCarbon.io, a SaaS platform for minting carbon credit NFTs via blockchain. In 2022, DeepMarkit acquired First Carbon Corp. (FCC), which operates the MintCarbon.io platform. The company ran test mints of carbon credit NFTs in 2022 and opened the platform publicly, though adoption remained limited as NFT markets cooled broadly.
DeepMarkit.summary_abuse: DeepMarkit operates in the speculative NFT and carbon credit tokenization space. NFT minting platforms have historically been associated with scam tokens and wash trading. Carbon credit NFT markets have faced criticism for lack of verification standards and potential greenwashing. DeepMarkit's micro-cap public company status and limited product traction raise concerns about viability.

deepMiner.history: deepMiner is an open-source JavaScript-based browser cryptocurrency miner released around 2017. It was designed to mine Monero and similar cryptocurrencies by running in website visitors' browsers without consent. It gained widespread notoriety when security researchers documented its extensive use in cryptojacking attacks.
deepMiner.summary_abuse: deepMiner is a JavaScript browser cryptominer with a very high abuse profile. It was specifically created to mine Monero by hijacking website visitors' CPU resources without their knowledge or consent. Security researchers documented its widespread deployment through compromised websites, often disguised under names mimicking jQuery. Cryptojacking with deepMiner-derived scripts was a major attack vector in 2017-2019. The project is effectively a cryptojacking tool, though it was technically open source.

Deko.history: Deko was founded around 2010 in London as Pay4Later, a UK retail finance technology platform for merchants. In 2017 Pay4Later rebranded as Deko. In 2020, NewDay (a UK credit card and finance company) acquired Deko.
Deko.summary_abuse: Deko is a UK retail finance and BNPL platform for merchants. As a FCA-regulated financial services provider it operates under strict lending compliance; no notable platform-specific fraud abuse has been documented beyond the general BNPL fraud risk inherent to any consumer lending product.

Delacon.history: Delacon was founded in 1996 in Sydney, Australia as a call management and tracking company. It has grown into a provider of call tracking, call management, and speech analytics solutions for Australian businesses.
Delacon.summary_abuse: Delacon is an Australian call tracking and speech analytics platform with no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B tool for marketing attribution and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface.

Delera.history: Delera is a marketing and sales automation platform. Limited public information is available about its founding history.
Delera.summary_abuse: Delera is a small marketing automation platform with no notable abuse signal.

Delight XR.history: Delight XR is a video platform providing AR and VR content delivery. Limited public founding history is available.
Delight XR.summary_abuse: Delight XR is a niche AR/VR video delivery platform with no notable abuse signal.

DelightChat.history: DelightChat was founded in 2020 by Preetam Nath and Sankalp Jonna as an omnichannel customer support and WhatsApp marketing platform for Shopify stores. It targets D2C e-commerce merchants.
DelightChat.summary_abuse: DelightChat is a customer support helpdesk for Shopify merchants with no notable abuse signal. It manages customer communications via WhatsApp and other channels and has no public content-hosting surface exploitable for fraud.

Delighted.history: Delighted was founded in 2013 in Palo Alto, California as a Net Promoter Score (NPS) and customer feedback SaaS. In April 2018, Qualtrics acquired Delighted to expand its CX measurement capabilities. The platform is being sunset by Qualtrics in June 2026.
Delighted.summary_abuse: Delighted is an NPS survey platform with no notable abuse signal. It collects customer satisfaction feedback and has no content-hosting or anonymous submission surface that fraudsters would exploit.

Delivengo.history: Delivengo is an international parcel shipping service operated by La Poste, the French national postal service. The service focuses on small parcels up to 2 kg for international e-commerce shipments, leveraging La Poste's global network and international postal partners to deliver to over 190 countries. The platform offers a Shopify app and integrates with major shipping software platforms including ShipStation and ShipEngine. Delivengo Easy is a variant targeting small e-commerce sellers with no minimum volume requirements.
Delivengo.summary_abuse: Delivengo is a shipping service from La Poste, France's national postal operator. As a carrier service, it can be used to ship counterfeit goods or illicit products, a risk common to all shipping carriers. La Poste cooperates with customs and law enforcement, and as a state-owned French company operates with strong regulatory oversight. No specific documented abuse incidents beyond the general risks applicable to all postal/parcel carriers.

Deliverr.history: Deliverr was founded in 2017 by Harish Abbott and Michael Krakaris to provide fast fulfillment for ecommerce merchants across platforms like Walmart, eBay, and Shopify. The company raised $490M in funding before being acquired by Shopify for $2.1 billion in May 2022. In June 2023, Shopify sold Deliverr along with its broader logistics business to Flexport in exchange for equity, making Flexport the current parent.
Deliverr.summary_abuse: Deliverr is a B2B fulfillment and logistics platform with no notable consumer-facing abuse surface. The service is used by legitimate ecommerce merchants and has no documented history of significant misuse for fraud, phishing, or illicit storefronts.

Delivra.history: Delivra was founded in 1999 in Indianapolis as an email marketing platform. In 2018 it was acquired by Campaign Monitor, then in December 2021 it was acquired again by Redbrick, a Canadian digital media holding company. Post-acquisition, Delivra launched a redesigned product called Delivra Air and expanded into SMS marketing automation.
Delivra.summary_abuse: Delivra is an email and SMS marketing platform. Email marketing platforms carry inherent abuse risk as they can be used for unsolicited bulk sending. Delivra serves legitimate SMB and nonprofit clients and maintains acceptable use policies, but its abuse surface is moderate given the mass-email capability. No specific notable incidents have been reported.

Delm.history: Delm is a Shopify app that displays estimated delivery dates and shipping information on product pages and cart to help merchants reduce WISMO inquiries and increase conversions. The app is a small-scale independent product targeting Shopify store owners.
Delm.summary_abuse: Delm is a small Shopify delivery date display app with no documented abuse incidents and a minimal consumer attack surface.

Delta Media.history: Delta Media Group traces its origins to 1993 when Wayne Mangold founded the company in Beaverton, Oregon, creating early online property search technology. After a rebranding to TigerFly and a move to California, Michael Minard and partners acquired and rebranded it again as Delta Media Group in 2000, moving to Barberton, Ohio. The company remains family-owned and CEO-operated with no outside investors, serving over 600 real estate brokerages with 35,000+ agents.
Delta Media.summary_abuse: Delta Media Group is a real estate technology platform for brokerages. It has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface and no documented misuse incidents.

Dema.history: Dema (dema.ai) was founded in 2022 in Stockholm, Sweden by Marcus Tagesson, Henrik Hoffman, and David Feldell. The company provides an AI-powered omnichannel commerce analytics platform for DTC and ecommerce brands, offering unified profitability measurement, marketing mix modeling, and AI-driven decision support.
Dema.summary_abuse: Dema is a B2B analytics platform for ecommerce brands. It processes customer and revenue data but serves legitimate DTC businesses. No documented abuse incidents have been reported.

Demandbase.history: Demandbase was founded in 2005 in San Francisco to provide B2B account-based marketing intelligence. It grew through major acquisitions including Engagio in 2019, and InsideView and DemandMatrix in 2021, evolving into a full account intelligence platform. By 2024 it reported $750M in revenue and had become a dominant ABM platform serving enterprise B2B go-to-market teams.
Demandbase.summary_abuse: Demandbase is a B2B marketing intelligence platform primarily used by enterprise sales and marketing teams. It collects and processes significant firmographic and intent data, raising privacy considerations around third-party data aggregation. No notable misuse for fraud or scams has been documented.

Demandforce.history: Demandforce was founded circa 2003 as a marketing automation platform for small businesses. Intuit acquired the company in 2012 for $423.5 million in cash, integrating it with QuickBooks for small business marketing. Intuit later divested Demandforce, which was acquired by Internet Brands and continues to serve the dental, medical, automotive, and service industries.
Demandforce.summary_abuse: Demandforce is a marketing automation and communications platform for small businesses including dental practices and auto repair shops. It sends email and SMS communications on behalf of clients, which carries some spam risk, but the platform targets regulated industries and requires business verification. No significant abuse incidents have been documented.

Demio.history: Demio was co-founded in 2014 by David Abrams and Wyatt Jozwowski as a browser-based webinar platform designed to eliminate the clunky download experience of existing tools. In 2021, Demio was acquired by Banzai International, a Seattle-area marketing technology company, and operates as a distinct product within Banzai's engagement marketing suite.
Demio.summary_abuse: Demio is a browser-based webinar platform used primarily by marketers for legitimate online events and lead generation. No notable abuse patterns specific to Demio have been documented. The platform requires account registration and has standard anti-abuse policies.

DenGro.history: DenGro is a CRM and lead management platform built for dental practices in the UK, later acquired by Agilio Software as part of its dental practice management portfolio. The platform automates patient enquiry handling, nurture communications, and integrates with dental practice management systems like EXACT and Dentally.
DenGro.summary_abuse: DenGro is a niche CRM for dental businesses handling patient enquiries. It operates in a regulated healthcare context with GDPR compliance and has no documented abuse surface or misuse patterns.

Deno.history: Deno was created by Ryan Dahl, original creator of Node.js, and announced at JSConf EU 2018 in a talk titled '10 Things I Regret About Node.js'. Version 1.0 was released on May 13, 2020. In March 2021, Deno Land Inc was incorporated with backing from Shasta Ventures and Mozilla Corporation to commercialize the runtime through the Deno Deploy cloud platform. Deno addresses design regrets from Node.js including adding native TypeScript support, a permission sandbox, and Web Standard APIs.
Deno.summary_abuse: Deno is a JavaScript and TypeScript runtime with a security-first architecture. Its permission sandbox model makes it less exploitable than Node.js for certain attack vectors. No notable abuse incidents have been reported; the runtime is used primarily by legitimate developers.

Deno Deploy.history: Deno Deploy is an edge computing and serverless platform launched in 2021 by Deno Land Inc, the company formed around the Deno JavaScript runtime created by Ryan Dahl. The platform allows deployment of JavaScript and TypeScript functions to a global edge network. It is the primary commercial offering that monetizes the open-source Deno runtime project.
Deno Deploy.summary_abuse: Deno Deploy is an edge serverless platform with free-tier hosting, which carries some risk of abuse for hosting malicious scripts or phishing content. However, the platform requires account registration and enforces usage policies. No significant documented abuse incidents have been reported.

Depict.history: Depict was founded in 2019 in Stockholm, Sweden as an AI-powered product discovery and recommendations platform for fashion ecommerce. The company participated in Y Combinator and raised a $17 million Series A led by Tiger Global with participation from EQT Ventures and Initialized Capital. It focuses on visual merchandising, smart search, and AI-driven collection grids for Shopify and Centra stores.
Depict.summary_abuse: Depict is a B2B AI merchandising and search platform for ecommerce retailers. It operates as a backend analytics and recommendation engine with no consumer-facing signup, presenting a minimal abuse surface. No documented abuse incidents have been reported.

DERAK.CLOUD.history: Derak Cloud was founded in 2018 in Shiraz, Iran as a cloud services provider offering CDN, cloud security, cloud servers, SSL certificates, big data, and Video on Demand streaming, primarily serving the Iranian and Middle Eastern markets.
DERAK.CLOUD.summary_abuse: DERAK.CLOUD is an Iranian cloud hosting and CDN provider. Infrastructure hosted in Iran faces elevated risk of sanction compliance issues and reduced international accountability for abuse. The vendor has a minimal international footprint and no documented public abuse-response track record. Iranian hosting providers in general are not subject to GDPR or Western data protection frameworks.

Deriv.history: Deriv traces its origins to 1999 when Regent Markets Group was founded in Hong Kong offering fixed-odds exchange deals, an early form of binary options. The company operated as Binary.com for over two decades, rebranding as Deriv in May 2020. It is regulated by the Malta Financial Services Authority among other authorities and offers CFDs, binary options, and synthetic indices across multiple trading platforms.
Deriv.summary_abuse: Deriv is an online trading platform specializing in binary options, CFDs, and synthetic indices. Binary options trading is banned in many jurisdictions due to its high-risk nature and association with consumer fraud. The platform allows demo accounts and has relatively low barriers to entry. Regulatory warnings about binary options platforms targeting retail investors have been issued in numerous countries, though Deriv holds licenses in Malta and other jurisdictions. The high-risk product category and retail-focused marketing elevate the abuse and consumer harm risk score.

Descartes.history: Descartes Systems Group was founded in 1981 in Waterloo, Ontario as a logistics software provider for route accounting. It went public on the Toronto Stock Exchange in 1998, later listing on NASDAQ. The company nearly went bankrupt in 2004 after a failed SaaS pivot but underwent a dramatic turnaround under CEO Arthur Mesher in 2005. Since then, Descartes has completed over 50 acquisitions, growing into a global logistics network serving 24,000+ customers with $500M+ revenue.
Descartes.summary_abuse: Descartes Systems is an enterprise B2B logistics platform with no consumer-facing surface. It serves global supply chain and trade compliance operations. No documented abuse incidents have been reported; the platform is used exclusively by large enterprises and governments for logistics automation.

Deskero.history: Deskero is an Italian cloud-based helpdesk software founded around 2013, providing multi-channel customer support that integrates email, chat, social media, and web forms. The company is independently operated from Italy and serves small-to-midsize businesses with an affordable per-agent pricing model.
Deskero.summary_abuse: Deskero is a niche Italian helpdesk SaaS with no documented abuse history. It serves small business customer support teams and has no notable consumer-facing attack surface.

DeskPro.history: DeskPro was founded in 2001 at Oxford University by Christopher Padfield and Christopher Nadeau, initially focused on helpdesk software. The company grew steadily over two decades offering both cloud and on-premise helpdesk solutions. In June 2024, Deskpro raised $25M in a Series A round led by Elsewhere Partners, appointed Brad Murdoch as CEO, and established a new global headquarters in Austin, Texas.
DeskPro.summary_abuse: DeskPro is a B2B helpdesk software platform used by enterprise support teams. It has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface and no documented misuse incidents. The self-hosted option and strong GDPR compliance posture make it suitable for regulated industries.

DeskPro Chat.history: DeskPro Chat is the live chat component of the DeskPro helpdesk platform, developed by the same company founded in 2001 at Oxford University. It functions as an integrated chat module within the broader DeskPro support suite and shares the same ownership and infrastructure.
DeskPro Chat.summary_abuse: DeskPro Chat is a live chat module embedded within enterprise helpdesk deployments. It has no consumer-facing open signup and no documented abuse surface beyond standard enterprise software risks.

Desku.history: Desku was founded in 2021 by Gaurav Nagani in Rajkot, India as an AI-powered customer service helpdesk platform. The company raised $120K from TinySeed and targets SaaS and ecommerce businesses with affordable support tooling.
Desku.summary_abuse: Desku is a small-scale AI helpdesk SaaS with no documented abuse patterns. The platform serves ecommerce and SaaS businesses and has a minimal consumer-facing surface.

Destinet.history: Destinet (DestiNet) was established in 2004 by ECOTRANS as a knowledge networking portal for sustainable and responsible tourism. It was registered as a UN Partnership for Sustainable Development in 2015 and re-launched in 2017 as Tourism2030, supporting the UN's 2030 Agenda for sustainable tourism development globally.
Destinet.summary_abuse: Destinet is a sustainable tourism knowledge portal maintained by a non-profit entity with UN partnership status. No abuse surface or misuse patterns have been documented.

Destini.history: Destini was founded in 2012 as a product locator and store-level data intelligence platform for CPG brands. The company developed the largest centralized product availability database in the US and became a subsidiary of SPINS, a wellness data provider, expanding to serve over 1,500 CPG brands across 150,000 retail locations.
Destini.summary_abuse: Destini is a B2B retail data and product locator platform serving CPG brands. It operates in the supply chain data space with no consumer-facing open-access features and no documented abuse incidents.

DESTOON.history: DESTOON B2B CMS was first released in early 2007 as an open-source PHP and MySQL based B2B portal and e-commerce website management system for the Chinese market. It evolved through multiple major versions, reaching version 3 in 2011 and commercial version 8 and personal edition version 7 in later years. The system supports Chinese payment integrations including Alipay and WeChat Pay.
DESTOON.summary_abuse: DESTOON is a self-hosted Chinese B2B CMS platform. Self-hosted CMS platforms like DESTOON can be deployed by operators who may create fraudulent storefronts or counterfeit goods marketplaces, and the Chinese B2B portal niche has historically seen such deployments. However the software itself is a neutral tool; abuse depends on how operators deploy it. No specific widespread abuse campaigns are attributed to DESTOON specifically.

Desty.history: Desty is an Indonesian merchant solutions platform founded around 2020, offering link-in-bio tools, online store creation, digital menus, and omnichannel dashboard capabilities. The company raised over $10 million in pre-Series A funding and grew to nearly a million users, targeting Indonesian SMB merchants and content creators.
Desty.summary_abuse: Desty is a link-in-bio and ecommerce platform primarily used by Indonesian small businesses. Link-in-bio platforms have moderate abuse potential as they can be used to redirect traffic to scam sites or phishing pages, but Desty primarily serves legitimate merchants in the Indonesian market with no specific documented abuse incidents.

Deta.history: Deta was founded in 2019 by Mustafa Abdelhai and Max Eusterbrock with offices in Berlin and New York. Initially launched as a developer cloud platform with free tools including Deta Base (NoSQL database) and Deta Drive, the company pivoted in 2023 towards Deta Space, a cloud-based personal operating system. In October 2023 it raised a $3.6 million pre-seed from Crane Venture Partners.
Deta.summary_abuse: Deta is a developer-focused cloud platform offering free-tier hosting and databases. Free-tier cloud platforms with custom-app hosting carry some abuse risk, but Deta's developer-centric positioning and requirement for account registration mitigate this. No specific abuse incidents have been documented.

Detectify.history: Detectify was founded in 2013 in Stockholm by a group of leading ethical hackers including Johan Edholm, Fredrik Nordberg Almroth, Mathias Karlsson, Rickard Carlsson, and David Hallenius. The company raised $42.1M from Insight Partners, Inventure, and Balderton Capital. In October 2024, Insight Partners acquired a majority stake. Clients include Trello, Spotify, and King.
Detectify.summary_abuse: Detectify is a web application security scanner and attack surface management platform used by security teams to find vulnerabilities. As a defensive security tool it has no notable consumer-facing attack surface or documented misuse incidents. Its ethical-hacker community model is considered a positive trust signal.

Detectizr.history: Detectizr is a Modernizr extension created to detect device, device model, screen size, operating system, and browser details. The last version published was 2.2.0, over ten years ago, making the project effectively abandoned. It was used by a small number of projects in the npm registry but has been superseded by more actively maintained alternatives like UAParser.js and device-detector-js.
Detectizr.summary_abuse: Detectizr is an abandoned JavaScript device detection library with no consumer-facing surface. It has no documented abuse incidents.

DEUNA.history: DEUNA was founded in 2020 by Roberto Kafati and Jose Maria Serrano in Silicon Valley, focused on AI-powered payment orchestration and commerce performance for merchants in Latin America. In July 2022 the company raised $37M in Series A funding to expand its digital commerce services across LatAm.
DEUNA.summary_abuse: DEUNA is a payment orchestration platform targeting Latin American merchants. As a payments intermediary it is subject to fraud monitoring requirements but has no notable documented abuse signal; legitimate use dominates and the company operates under standard payment industry compliance.

Deutsche Post.history: Deutsche Post was formed from the German Federal postal service and privatized in 1995. It listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange in 2000 and merged with DHL in 2002, forming Deutsche Post DHL Group, now one of the world's largest logistics companies operating in 220 countries and territories.
Deutsche Post.summary_abuse: Deutsche Post is a major national postal and logistics provider. Its tracking and delivery notification systems are occasionally spoofed in phishing campaigns that impersonate Deutsche Post to steal credentials, a problem common to all major postal services. Deutsche Post operates actively to combat such impersonation and fraud.

Develic Omnium Maps.history: Omnium Maps by Develic Solutions is a Shopify store locator app launched on March 15, 2017, by a small team based in Tallinn, Estonia. It provides Google Maps integration with support for up to 2000 store locations per map and is sold on the Shopify App Store.
Develic Omnium Maps.summary_abuse: Omnium Maps is a small Shopify store locator app with no documented abuse incidents and a minimal consumer-facing attack surface.

Developing Azerbaijan.history: Developing Azerbaijan appears to be associated with Azerbaijan's digital development initiatives coordinated by the Innovation and Digital Development Agency (IDDA). The Azerbaijani government has pursued digital transformation programs including the Digital Azerbaijan initiative to modernize public services and encourage private sector digital adoption.
Developing Azerbaijan.summary_abuse: Limited information is available about this technology. Based on its apparent connection to Azerbaijani digital development initiatives, no specific abuse incidents have been documented.

DevExtreme.history: DevExtreme is a JavaScript UI components library developed by DevExpress, a company founded in 1997 in Las Vegas, Nevada. DevExtreme was launched around 2011 as a cross-platform HTML5/JavaScript UI framework for building data-intensive business applications. It supports Angular, React, Vue, and jQuery and is widely used in enterprise line-of-business applications.
DevExtreme.summary_abuse: DevExtreme is a JavaScript UI component library for enterprise applications. As a development toolkit it has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse incidents.

DevRev.history: DevRev was founded in October 2020 by Dheeraj Pandey and Manoj Agarwal, veterans from Nutanix. The company raised over $85 million in seed funding from Khosla Ventures and Mayfield, the largest seed in Silicon Valley history at the time. DevRev reached unicorn status in 2024 with its AI-first OneCRM platform designed to connect product, support, and development teams.
DevRev.summary_abuse: DevRev is a B2B CRM and support platform targeting SaaS companies. It has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface and no documented misuse incidents.

Devuelving.history: Devuelving is a Spanish ecommerce returns management platform targeting online retailers. Limited public information is available about its founding details; the company operates from Spain and provides returns workflow automation for ecommerce merchants.
Devuelving.summary_abuse: Devuelving is a niche Spanish ecommerce returns platform with no documented abuse incidents. It serves legitimate online retailers with returns workflow management.

Devup UI.history: Devup UI is a small UI component library or design tool. Minimal public information is available about its founding or company details.
Devup UI.summary_abuse: Devup UI appears to be a niche developer UI tool with no documented abuse history or notable consumer-facing attack surface.

DevzCart.history: DevzCart is an all-in-one ecommerce and business management platform based in Bangladesh, providing product management, order management, inventory tracking, multi-channel sales, and courier integrations specifically for Bangladeshi merchants. It targets small to large ecommerce businesses in the local market.
DevzCart.summary_abuse: DevzCart is a regional ecommerce platform for Bangladeshi merchants. No notable abuse incidents have been documented. As a local platform with integrated courier and payment systems, its abuse surface is primarily limited to standard ecommerce fraud risks.

Dexem.history: Dexem was founded in 2000 in Chantepie (near Rennes), France by Alain Jaouen, Philippe Simon, and Eric Ravelomanantsoa. The company provides cloud-based call tracking, IVR, and telephony solutions, handling over 200 million calls per year. Clients include Air France, Accor, Renault-Nissan, Carrefour, and BNP Paribas. The company remains independently funded with around 16 employees.
Dexem.summary_abuse: Dexem is a French B2B call tracking and telephony platform serving enterprise clients. No documented abuse incidents have been reported. Phone number tracking services carry minimal abuse risk in a B2B context.

Dexie.js.history: Dexie.js is an open-source JavaScript wrapper for IndexedDB, created by David Fahlander. It simplifies the IndexedDB API with a promise-based interface, TypeScript support, and bulk operation performance optimizations. The library is used by over 100,000 websites and apps. The maintainer also offers Dexie Cloud, a commercial sync service built on top of the library.
Dexie.js.summary_abuse: Dexie.js is a browser-side IndexedDB wrapper library with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse incidents. As a pure client-side utility library it has zero abuse potential.

Dexter.history: Dexter was a chatbot and conversational AI building platform founded around 2015. The platform allowed users to build bots for various messaging channels. Limited public information is available about its current status.
Dexter.summary_abuse: Dexter is a chatbot building platform with limited current activity. Chatbot platforms can be used to build spam or phishing bots, but no specific incidents tied to Dexter have been documented.

DHL.history: DHL was founded in 1969 in San Francisco by Adrian Dalsey, Larry Hillblom, and Robert Lynn, pioneering door-to-door international air express delivery between San Francisco and Honolulu. The company expanded globally through the 1970s and 1980s. Deutsche Post acquired DHL in stages between 2001 and 2002, fully integrating it as a division. Deutsche Post is publicly traded and DHL operates in over 220 countries and territories worldwide.
DHL.summary_abuse: DHL is one of the world's largest logistics companies and a major target for brand impersonation in phishing campaigns. Attackers frequently create fraudulent DHL delivery notification emails and fake tracking pages to steal credentials and payment details. DHL itself is a legitimate operator but its brand recognition makes it one of the most impersonated companies in phishing globally. DHL has an active brand protection team and works with authorities to combat impersonation, but the sheer scale of abuse attempts targeting its brand warrants an elevated score.

DHTMLX.history: DHTMLX was launched around 2005 by the Lithuanian company Dinamenta, UAB, with the first components dhtmlxTree and dhtmlxGrid. Over the following two decades the library expanded to cover scheduling, Gantt charting, diagrams, kanban boards and a comprehensive UI suite. The library is available under both a GPL open-source license and a commercial license, serving industries including aviation, healthcare, and construction.
DHTMLX.summary_abuse: DHTMLX is a self-hosted JavaScript UI component library for building web applications. It has no consumer-facing surface and no abuse signal, as it is purely a developer toolkit running in the user's own application.

Dialog Insight.history: Dialog Insight was founded in 1999 in Quebec City, Canada by Pascale Guay, who continues as CEO. The company originally launched as an email marketing platform and evolved into a customer engagement and marketing automation platform serving enterprise and mid-market clients in Canada and France. The platform has over 75 employees across both countries.
Dialog Insight.summary_abuse: Dialog Insight is a Canadian email and marketing automation platform. Email marketing platforms have inherent mass-communication abuse potential, but Dialog Insight serves mid-market enterprises in regulated industries and maintains anti-spam compliance policies. No specific abuse incidents have been documented.

Dialogity.history: Dialogity is a WhatsApp and messaging automation platform for businesses. Minimal public information is available about its founding details or company history beyond its positioning as a conversational marketing tool.
Dialogity.summary_abuse: Dialogity is a niche WhatsApp automation platform. WhatsApp marketing tools have moderate spam potential but no specific abuse incidents tied to Dialogity have been documented.

DialogShift.history: DialogShift was founded in 2019 in Berlin, Germany, and has become the German market leader in AI-powered guest communication for hotels. The platform uses OpenAI and Anthropic models via Microsoft Azure and AWS, achieving over 95% automation rates. By 2024 it supported more than 1,200 hotel clients including Motel One.
DialogShift.summary_abuse: DialogShift is a hotel chatbot AI platform with no documented consumer-facing abuse surface. It operates exclusively in the hospitality B2B sector and maintains GDPR compliance under European law.

DialogTab.history: DialogTab is a customer messaging and live chat platform. Minimal public information is available about its founding details or company history.
DialogTab.summary_abuse: DialogTab is a niche live chat platform with no documented abuse incidents or notable consumer-facing risk.

Dialogue.history: Dialogue is a Canadian virtual healthcare and employee wellness platform founded in 2016 in Montreal. The company provides primary and preventive care, mental health, and wellness programs through an app. It trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange and serves employers looking to provide healthcare benefits to their workforce.
Dialogue.summary_abuse: Dialogue is a virtual healthcare platform operating in a regulated healthcare context in Canada. It handles health data with appropriate privacy practices. No documented abuse incidents have been reported.

DiamondCDN.history: DiamondCDN was a free CDN with DDoS mitigation service run by Edward Coristine, who operated it while still in high school around 2021-2022. Coristine later became notable as a DOGE associate. The service was a micro-CDN project with a small user base and no notable commercial track record.
DiamondCDN.summary_abuse: DiamondCDN is a very small free CDN with DDoS protection. Free CDN services without rigorous KYC or abuse controls can be used to proxy malicious content or protect hostile infrastructure from takedown. The operator's later association with controversial public activities adds reputational risk. Abuse potential is moderate given the free-tier, no-KYC model.

Dianomi.history: Dianomi was incorporated in August 2002 in England and Wales as Data-ID Limited, renamed Dianomi Limited in December 2002. Founded by Rupert Hodson, Raphael Queisser, and Cabell de Marcellus, the company operates a premium native advertising marketplace connecting financial services advertisers with publishers like The Wall Street Journal, Reuters, and Kiplinger. Dianomi listed on the AIM market of the London Stock Exchange in May 2021.
Dianomi.summary_abuse: Dianomi is a native advertising marketplace focused on premium financial services content. As an ad network it embeds tracking and targeting across publisher sites, carrying the typical ad-tech privacy concerns. It targets high-intent professional audiences rather than mass-market consumers, and operates under London Stock Exchange listing requirements and GDPR. No specific fraud or scam incidents have been documented.

Dice.history: Dice.com was founded in 1990 as a tech jobs platform. It became one of the earliest online job boards for technology professionals. The company is owned by DHI Group, Inc., a publicly traded company. Dice primarily focuses on US tech job listings for software engineers, IT professionals, and related roles.
Dice.summary_abuse: Dice is a technology job board where abuse primarily takes the form of fake job postings used to collect resumes and personal data, which is a known issue across all job platforms. Dice has standard fraud reporting mechanisms but like all job sites faces ongoing challenges with scam recruiters and fake postings.

Didar.history: Didar is an Iranian CRM and sales management platform founded around 2019, targeting small and medium businesses in the Persian-speaking market with customer relationship management, lead tracking, and sales pipeline tools.
Didar.summary_abuse: Didar is a niche CRM platform serving the Iranian market. No documented abuse incidents have been reported. The platform operates in a limited geographic market with standard business software characteristics.

Didomi.history: Didomi was founded in 2017 in Paris by Raphael Boukris and Romain Gauthier as a consent management platform. The company raised $45.8M in funding and expanded to operate in 35 countries serving over 1,500 clients. It has grown to approximately 165 employees across 6 continents and has been recognized as a privacy-by-design compliance leader.
Didomi.summary_abuse: Didomi is a consent management platform designed specifically to help organizations comply with GDPR and other privacy regulations. As a privacy-focused tool, it has an inherently low abuse surface and is used to reduce rather than enable tracking or data misuse. No documented abuse incidents have been reported.

Digest.history: Digest is a content curation or newsletter tool for internal team communications. Minimal public information is available about its founding details or company history.
Digest.summary_abuse: Digest appears to be a niche productivity tool. No documented abuse incidents have been reported.

DigiCDN.history: DigiCDN is a small content delivery network provider. Minimal public information is available about its founding, ownership, or corporate history.
DigiCDN.summary_abuse: DigiCDN is a small CDN service. Small CDN providers with limited abuse controls can be used to proxy malicious content, but no specific incidents tied to DigiCDN have been documented.

DigiCert.history: DigiCert was founded in 2003 by Ken Bretschneider in Lehi, Utah. In 2017 it acquired Symantec's SSL and PKI certificate business for $950 million, gaining control of GeoTrust, Thawte, RapidSSL, and VeriSign brands. This acquisition followed browser distrust of Symantec-issued certificates by Google and Mozilla, making DigiCert the world's leading certificate authority serving the largest websites globally.
DigiCert.summary_abuse: DigiCert is the world's leading certificate authority. While TLS certificates have historically been misused by phishing sites to appear legitimate, DigiCert operates the CA/Browser Forum validation standards and maintains strict validation practices. It responds promptly to certificate revocation requests. The company itself is not a source of abuse but its infrastructure is part of the global trust chain.

DigiFi.history: DigiFi was founded in 2017 in New York by Matthew Norton, Bradley Vanderstarren, and Joshua Jersey. In 2019 it launched the world's first open-source loan origination system, making core lending infrastructure available to banks, credit unions, and fintech lenders. The platform handles consumer, mortgage, small business, and commercial lending workflows.
DigiFi.summary_abuse: DigiFi is a loan origination system used by regulated financial institutions. It handles sensitive financial data but operates in a supervised lending context. No documented abuse incidents have been reported.

Digioh.history: Digioh was founded in 2011 by Robert Steffes in San Francisco. The company provides an identity-first personalization platform for ecommerce brands including guided selling quizzes, dynamic content, and lead capture tools. The company bootstrapped without external funding and serves over 2,000 DTC brands.
Digioh.summary_abuse: Digioh is a lead capture and personalization platform embedded on ecommerce sites. It collects email addresses and user behavior data for marketing purposes. The platform requires business registration and its abuse surface is limited to standard lead capture and email marketing risks. No specific abuse incidents have been documented.

Digismoothie Candy Rack.history: Candy Rack by Digismoothie is a Shopify upsell and cross-sell app developed by a Czech company. It enables merchants to display pre-purchase and in-cart upsell offers to increase average order value. The app is independently operated and sold through the Shopify App Store.
Digismoothie Candy Rack.summary_abuse: Candy Rack is a Shopify upsell app designed to encourage additional purchases at checkout. While upsell apps can employ persuasive design, Candy Rack operates within Shopify's ecosystem rules. No specific abuse incidents have been documented, though aggressive upsell patterns are inherent to this product category.

Digistore24.history: Digistore24 was founded in 2012 in Germany as a digital product marketplace and affiliate network. The platform acts as a reseller for vendors selling online courses, e-books, coaching, and digital products, handling taxes, invoicing, and affiliate payouts. It has grown into one of Europe's largest affiliate marketplaces with over 8,500 offers.
Digistore24.summary_abuse: Digistore24 is a digital product marketplace with a large affiliate network. The platform is known to host a high proportion of questionable get-rich-quick schemes, weight loss products with dubious claims, and low-quality info products promoted through aggressive affiliate marketing. While Digistore24 is technically a legitimate company and handles GDPR compliance, its lax vetting of product claims and affiliate tactics has attracted regulatory scrutiny and consumer complaints in Germany and the EU. The affiliate model incentivizes misleading promotional content.

Digital Showroom.history: Digital Showroom is an Indian ecommerce platform enabling small businesses to create online stores and sell through WhatsApp, social media, and websites. The platform targets micro-merchants and small retailers in India seeking to transition to digital commerce.
Digital Showroom.summary_abuse: Digital Showroom is a micro-merchant ecommerce platform in India. As an open-signup platform enabling custom storefronts, it carries some risk of being used for fake or low-quality storefronts, but no specific documented abuse incidents have been reported.

DigitalOcean Spaces.history: DigitalOcean Spaces is an S3-compatible object storage service launched by DigitalOcean, a cloud platform founded in 2011 in New York by Ben and Moisey Uretsky. DigitalOcean went public on NYSE in March 2021 and acquired Cloudways for $350M in 2022. Spaces was introduced as part of DigitalOcean's product expansion beyond its core virtual machine offering, providing a developer-friendly and affordable alternative to AWS S3.
DigitalOcean Spaces.summary_abuse: DigitalOcean Spaces is a cloud object storage service. Like all cloud storage services it can be used to host malicious files or phishing assets, but DigitalOcean maintains AUP enforcement and abuse reporting channels. Cloud storage abuse is an industry-wide issue and DigitalOcean responds to takedown requests in a manner consistent with major cloud providers.

DigitalRiver.history: Digital River was founded in 1994 by Joel Ronning in Minnesota to enable software companies to sell products online. It went public on Nasdaq in 1998 and grew through numerous acquisitions to process over $30B in online transactions by 2013. Siris Capital took the company private for $840M in 2015. The company suffered payment failures to merchants in 2024, ceased operations and filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy in May 2025.
DigitalRiver.summary_abuse: Digital River filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy in May 2025 after ceasing operations. Prior to closure, merchants reported non-payment of owed funds. The platform has been associated with unauthorized charges and difficulties cancelling subscriptions by end consumers. As the company has effectively shut down, it represents a significant vendor risk rather than an active abuse vector.

Digizuite.history: Digizuite is a Danish Digital Asset Management platform founded in 2002, providing enterprise DAM, media management, and content distribution solutions. The company is based in Copenhagen and serves enterprise clients in the Nordic region and beyond.
Digizuite.summary_abuse: Digizuite is an enterprise Digital Asset Management platform. As a B2B tool for managing corporate media assets, it has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface or documented misuse incidents.

digi·access.history: digi·access is a German accessibility SaaS company based in Leverkusen with over 10 years of experience helping organizations comply with WCAG 2.1/2.2 and European accessibility standards including EN 301 549 and BITV. It offers automated remediation tools and monitoring for websites.
digi·access.summary_abuse: digi·access has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B web accessibility compliance SaaS used by European companies and public organizations to meet legal accessibility requirements, with no consumer-facing surface that fraudsters would target.

Dimensions AI.history: Dimensions AI was launched in 2018 by Digital Science, a technology company owned by Holtzbrinck Publishing Group. The platform was built in collaboration with over 100 research funders and universities to create a linked research discovery database. As of 2023 it covers nearly 140 million publications with over 1.8 billion citations, plus grants, patents, and clinical trials.
Dimensions AI.summary_abuse: Dimensions AI is an academic research database used by institutions and researchers. It handles citation metadata and grant data with no notable consumer-facing abuse surface. No documented misuse or abuse incidents have been reported.
